summary: Frontend testing completed for Employee document upload feature. All 4 document upload fields (Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Qualification Certificate, Guardian/Father Consent Letter) are present in Add/Edit Employee modal. File type validation (PDF, JPG, PNG) and size validation (5MB max) working correctly on both frontend and backend.
passed_tests: UI: Add Employee modal shows 'Upload Employee Documents' section header
UI: Description text 'Upload Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Qualification Certificate, and Guardian Consent Letter (PDF, JPG, PNG - Max 5MB each)' visible
UI: Aadhar Card * upload field with 'Choose Aadhar Card File' button visible
UI: PAN Card * upload field with 'Choose PAN Card File' button visible
UI: Qualification Certificate upload field with 'Choose Certificate File' button visible
UI: Guardian/Father Consent Letter upload field with 'Choose Consent Letter' button visible
UI: All 4 file inputs have correct accept attribute (.pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png)
UI: Edit Employee modal shows 'Upload Employee Documents' section
UI: Edit Employee modal shows 'Uploaded Documents:' section with existing docs (green background)
Integration: File selection works (shows file name with blue background)
Integration: Remove button (X icon) visible for selected file
Integration: File removal restores upload button
Backend: POST /api/employees/{id}/upload returns 200 with file metadata
Backend: GET /api/employees/{id}/files returns list of uploaded documents
Backend: File type validation rejects invalid types (returns 400 with error message)
critical_code_review_comments: Good: Document upload section properly placed after ID Proofs section in modal (line 1411-1600)
Good: File type validation on frontend (handleDocumentSelect function, line 336-340)
Good: File size validation on frontend (5MB max, line 343-346)
Good: Proper FormData usage for file upload (uploadDocument function, line 355-374)
Good: Documents uploaded after employee creation (handleSubmit, line 297-303)
Good: Existing documents shown when editing (fetchEmployeeDocuments called in openEdit, line 419)
Good: Hidden file inputs with refs for all 4 document types (lines 114-117)
Good: Color-coded document type icons (blue for Aadhar, orange for PAN, green for Certificate, purple for Consent)
seed_data_creation: Uploaded test aadhar_card document to employee Rahul Sharma (ID: 8a329798-7c2d-4114-b6e6-b0a75f80a7cd)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Document upload feature fully verified. Add Employee modal contains 4 document upload fields under 'Upload Employee Documents' section. Edit Employee modal shows existing uploaded documents in green background section. Backend endpoints /api/employees/{id}/upload and /api/employees/{id}/files working correctly. Files stored in /app/uploads/employees/{employee_id}/ with metadata in employee_files collection.

document_upload_section.description: Upload Aadhar Card, PAN Card, Qualification Certificate, and Guardian Consent Letter (PDF, JPG, PNG - Max 5MB each)

edit_employee_modal.existing_docs_section: Green background with 'Uploaded Documents:' header and file badges
